title: Refactor /api/enrich-comments/stream to parallel comment building
description: Fan out comment-string building (analysis/comment.py) through the shared ThreadPool. DjmdContent.Commnt writes stay on a single writer thread; the per-track commit pattern documented in CLAUDE.md is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/enrich-comments/stream produces identical resulting comment strings compared to the v0 serial path on the same library.
The DB backup is still made once up-front before any writes (no change in safety semantics).
Per-track exception emits an error event and decrements 'enriched' as before; undo_data still returns {modified: [...]}.
/api/enrich-comments/undo continues to fully reverse a run.

steps.details: In analysis/comment.py pull build_comment_string + the per-track decision logic into _build_comment_for_track(content, db, prefs) returning {content_id, new_comment, previous_comment}.
In serve/routes.py /api/enrich-comments/stream handler: backup first (unchanged), then pool.submit _build_comment_for_track per content. Drain results into a queue.Queue.

Writer thread: for each result, write DjmdContent.Commnt = new_comment, db.commit(), append {content_id, previous: previous_comment} to undo_data, emit SSE progress event. On per-track exception: rollback that single track, increment errors, decrement enriched (existing semantics).

Update tests/test_serve_routes.py enrich-comments cases. Add test: 100 tracks, 1 raises - 99 enriched + 1 error, undo restores 99 tracks to their previous comment. Also assert MAX_COMMENT_LEN cap (256) still enforced and user-only-text-over-cap tracks still skipped (no truncation of user text).
RUN_PERF=1 pytest tests/perf/test_enrich.py - confirm >=100 tracks/sec warm.

technicalNotes: The /* AutoCue: ... */ sentinel-block convention must be preserved exactly (idempotent rewrite). String building is pure - safe in any thread.
User-authored text is never trimmed: when user text alone exceeds MAX_COMMENT_LEN, the writer must skip the track.
